front: Film: two women repeatedly enter a mysterious haunted house in Paris, emerging each time with a hard candy in their mouths and no memory; they suck on the candies to remember
back: Celine and Julie Go Boating (Jacques Rivette)
work: Celine and Julie Go Boating (Céline et Julie vont en bateau, 1974)

front: Film: opens with Julie dropping her scarf like the White Rabbit from Alice in Wonderland; Celine chases her (including onto a tram) for 15 minutes
back: Celine and Julie Go Boating (Jacques Rivette)
work: Celine and Julie Go Boating (Céline et Julie vont en bateau, 1974)

front: Film: stars Juliet Berto (Celine) and Dominique Labourier (Julie), who moved in together during production and recalled their dreams to get into character
back: Celine and Julie Go Boating (Jacques Rivette)
work: Celine and Julie Go Boating (Céline et Julie vont en bateau, 1974)

front: Film: 13-hour epic inspired by Balzac's Human Comedy; first episode titled 'From Lili to Thomas'; used 16mm for TV crew footage and 35mm for the crew themselves; first publicly shown in the U.S. in 2006, 35 years after its premiere
Film: depicts the breakdown of the relationship between Sébastien (directing Racine's Andromaque) and his wife Claire (playing Hermione), who can't stand the TV crew filming rehearsals
Film: four-hour film based on Balzac's 'The Unknown Masterpiece' — about painter Frenhofer who destroys his unfinished masterpiece
Director: famous essay condemned Gillo Pontecorvo's Kapo for 'tilting the camera up' to Emmanuelle Riva's dying hand — called it deserving 'nothing but contempt'
Director: led a putsch against Eric Rohmer to become head editor of Cahiers du Cinéma, taking it in a Marxist direction; also wrote 'The Genius of Howard Hawks'
Director: called Showgirls 'one of the great American films of the last few years'
